title: QUIC RFC 9001 Repeated Epoch Ownership Architecture
purpose: Define the design boundary for the repeated epoch ownership model after the representative later-epoch cleanup floor, including explicit current, next, and retained-old ownership, per-epoch packet-number floors, and sender/recovery cleanup synchronization.
risks: If current, next, and retained-old ownership blur together, repeated cleanup can clear live epoch state.
If sender/recovery cleanup is not synchronized with retained-old discard, old phases can linger or be double-cleared.
If representative later-epoch coverage is mistaken for full unbounded proof, the follow-on can overstate completion.
design_summary: The runtime already owns the repeated epoch facts in QuicOneRttKeyUpdateLifecycle and publishes them through QuicTransportTlsBridgeState. This slice keeps current, next, and retained-old ownership explicit, preserves current-phase acknowledgment and repeated-update cooldown ownership for the active epoch, and keeps retained-old discard synchronized with sender/recovery cleanup rather than widening the TLS bridge into a generic unbounded ledger. Representative later-epoch property coverage across phases 6 through 32 remains the bounded evidence floor alongside the direct lifecycle requirement-home proof.
alternatives_considered: Fold repeated ownership into the first-successor floor. Rejected because it would hide later-epoch behavior behind an unrelated prerequisite.
Model the unbounded ledger only in tests. Rejected because the runtime already has a dedicated lifecycle seam and the ownership boundaries should be traceable in architecture.
Treat representative later-epoch coverage as full proof. Rejected because it would overstate completion.
edge_cases_and_constraints: Current-phase acknowledgment and repeated-update cooldown ownership must survive retained-old cleanup.
Packet-number floors must remain distinct per retained/current/next epoch boundary.
Retained-old discard must clear only the retained old epoch and matching sender/recovery state.
Representative later-epoch coverage must remain bounded evidence, not a claim of exhaustiveness.
The repeated epoch model must remain separate from the stateless-reset response matrix.
data_and_state_considerations: The lifecycle object keeps retained old open/protect material, retained next open material, the retained old discard deadline, the retained old key phase, the repeated-update cooldown deadline, and the acknowledged current key phase. The sender and recovery ledgers tag repeated 1-RTT packets by retained old key phase so the runtime can expire the correct old epoch without clearing current packet-protection material or current-phase ownership. The direct lifecycle slice exercises that ownership model alongside the existing representative later-epoch proof without introducing a broader unbounded ledger.
open_questions: If a future phase range needs a distinct epoch identifier or packet-number floor model, split that out instead of growing the retained-old cleanup branch.
